definitions: A unit of length equal to 10⁻¹⁰ meter, 0.1 nanometer, or 100 picometers. It is often used to express the size of atoms, molecules, and the wavelengths of electromagnetic radiation.
examples: The bond length was measured in angstroms.
One angstrom is a very small unit.
X-rays have wavelengths on the order of an angstrom.
